title: larix olgensis var. koreana
intro: Larix olgensis var. koreana, also known as Korean larch, is a deciduous coniferous tree native to Korea and northeastern China, characterized by its narrow, pointed needles and red-brown bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Korean larch include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as possessing potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and needles of the tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
